title: Path variable regex can't match URL-encoded slashes even with UseEncodedPath
body: newRouteRegexp in regexp.go builds path variable patterns using the default `[^/]+`, which excludes `/`. Even when the router is configured with UseEncodedPath, a path segment containing an encoded slash (%2F) never matches a `{var}` placeholder because the underlying regex still treats it as a separator after decoding assumptions elsewhere in matching.

title: CORSMethodMiddleware doesn't let you customize allowed headers
body: middleware.go's CORSMethodMiddleware automatically reflects the route's allowed methods into Access-Control-Allow-Methods, which is great, but there's no equivalent way to configure Access-Control-Allow-Headers for routes that need custom request headers (e.g. Authorization, X-Request-Id).

title: 405 falls back silently to 404 when only NotFoundHandler is set
body: If a Router only sets NotFoundHandler (not MethodNotAllowedHandler) and a request matches a route's path but not its method, I'd expect ErrMethodMismatch handling to still return 405 using the default method-not-allowed behavior, but it looks like mux.go routes it through the 404 path instead in that configuration.

title: Path() vs PathPrefix() precedence undocumented when both could match
body: route.go documents Path() and PathPrefix() individually, but not what happens when a router has both a Path("/users") route and a PathPrefix("/users") route registered — does registration order alone decide, or is there additional specificity-based matching? A short doc note would save people from trial-and-error.

title: old_test.go overlaps with mux_test.go, unclear what's still relevant
body: old_test.go appears to predate a lot of the coverage now in mux_test.go, and several test names/cases look duplicated in spirit. It's hard for a new contributor to know whether old_test.go documents legacy behavior that must be preserved, or whether it can be merged/retired.
